Transcrição Completa: 2 Lessons from Making 6 Million in 18 Months Through Ecom with Jason
[00:00] Okay, guys. Uh, so this is my first time ever doing a presentation, so bear with me if I make mistakes. Um, so yeah, so I just wanted to put that catchy headline so I can grab your attentions, guys.
[00:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Chart Shopify Store Performance"
NÚMEROS: $6+ million, January até presente, 15 meses
VISUAL: Gráfico de linha ascendente mostrando crescimento de vendas, dashboard do Shopify com métricas de receita ao longo do tempo
[00:22] Um, so this is a chart from my Shopify store from January uh till a couple days ago. So you can tell that we hit like six a little bit, you know, uh million dollars in the past 15 months um selling fajas you know like Jeremy says uh shapewear for women's who had surgery.
[00:41] Uh, so it's very niche uh we're thinking to expand like a everyday use fajas for women you know so we definitely think that we can expand it and grow it to like multiple uh figures like probably like five to 10 million per month if we do the transition to every day.
[01:02] Uh, because it's like a mass market like skins like Spanx you know like like those are billion dollar brands you. Uh but we started like that because that was my my background for plastic surgery. I know that that's a must that people need it.
[01:20] Um so yeah, we actually took uh six months to produce that type of u shapewear because it's very um we actually did like a research uh read Amazon reviews to to fix problems.
[01:35] Uh have a very good um quality of products, you know, we pay extra uh for the cost of production. So we actually make sure it's a it's a good product, you know. So that's why we can also uh overcharge for it, you know.

NÚMEROS: Amazon market price: $40-$60, Nossa marca: $129.99-$160
VISUAL: Comparação visual de preços, mostrando diferencial de valor premium, destaque para pricing strategy
[01:55] Like market price on Amazon for a faja used to be like I don't know 40 to 60 bucks. We charge at a minimum of $129.99, you know, and we go up through $160.
[02:10] Uh so you know, it goes both hands, you know. Um but the thing I'm I'm trying to say with that I don't focus on Facebook ads that much is because I actually started a campaign on March 12, 2021. I haven't touched since.

NÚMEROS: $13,000 revenue last month, pode pagar 2 funcionários
VISUAL: Logo do app One Click Upsell, gráfico mostrando receita adicional gerada, ícone de dinheiro representando economia operacional
[03:40] Because $13,000, for example, in this example, which is one click upsell um can actually pay for two employees, you know, for the brand uh which can help uh make the the brand bigger, you know.
[03:55] So, uh it's something that we didn't have before, you know. So even like saving 10k, 15k in every app, it can it can make us um more money in the long run because we have a better team, a bigger team, you know, so we can we can do more stuff, you know.
[04:15] So this is a pretty good example like one click upsell. I only use it for post-purchase upsells because I found that like the post-purchase um uh pre-purchase upsells actually are super slow, at least in my in my in my case and in my shop.
[04:35] And then people just take too long to decide and then I I just saw like a conversion rate dropping a lot. So I just do it for like post purchase when they actually just purchase.
[04:48] Uh we still uh need to try the the down sells and the thank you offer, you know, but the pre-purchase upsell didn't work for me in this case.
[04:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Post-Purchase Upsell Strategy"
NÚMEROS: $13,000/mês, 10% discount offer
VISUAL: Fluxo visual mostrando jornada do cliente: compra inicial → oferta pós-compra → segunda faja com desconto, ícones de produtos e percentual de desconto
[05:05] Uh but um it made $13,000 last month just for post-purchase upsells, you know. Uh pretty much the the hook there is the selling another faja because in the case of surgery when you use a faja usually they got bloods and stings you know.
[05:25] Uh so they usually need to change back and forth between two fajas as that's that's why the hook actually is for the post purchase you know we give them an offer of uh 10% discount on that case so they can purchase another one uh either the same one or another color you if you want.

[06:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pricing Math: Accessories vs Discounts"
NÚMEROS: Accessory retail: $39.99, Cost: $3, 20% off faja: $26 loss, Net savings: $23
VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado com cálculos matemáticos, ícones de calculadora, setas mostrando economia, destaque para valor percebido vs custo real
[06:28] Um, a good thing, uh, a good way to think about it and I also do is just do the math and stuff. Um, for example, we can offer a product that is an accessory that we sell it on $39.99 to the to the end customers that actually cost us three bucks on China, you know.
[06:50] Uh, instead of giving like a 20% off, you know, like 20% off for a faja that is $129 is like $26 less money from us on the front end.
[07:02] Uh but if we offer a $39.99 product uh for free that they feel that they're getting 40 bucks, it's actually cost me only three bucks, you know, in China. They don't know that, you know, so it's actually way cheaper to offer accessories for free than 20% off. And it feels better for them, you know.
[07:25] Um that's how we're also making the email marketing campaigns and that's also what we're doing for the pop-ups to get more people onto our email marketing u funnel, you know. So yeah, always consider doing the math on what it cost uh to deliver something, you know.

[08:25] Same thing, you know, it just makes sense for us. Uh cost per acquisition is around 17% on Facebook. So just giving them 10% is still cheaper than actually uh doing acquisition by Facebook. No, we're saving up 7% in doing that, you know.
[08:45] Um so it just makes sense, you know. So, we brought $44,000 on that last month.

[18:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Shipping Signature Cost Analysis"
NÚMEROS: Signature cost: $2.25/order, Monthly cost: $10,600, Replacements: ~30-40/mês, Replacement cost: ~$1,500, Savings: ~$8,000
VISUAL: Tabela comparativa de custos, cálculo matemático detalhado, setas mostrando economia ao eliminar assinatura, destaque para ROI positivo da estratégia
[18:15] Um do the math. Yeah. So same thing I I was mentioning right it was a a perfect example of something that was happening in my store a couple months ago. Um, and then I probably all all people who has e-commerce uh are dealing with this is people saying that their package didn't didn't get to them, right?
[18:38] Um, they didn't receive it or or it says delivered but they didn't got it. Uh, I know that you can go to the traditional way of asking for insurance and stuff but that's super long and then it's only up to $100. Our fajas are like way more expensive but sometimes they they have two fajas right.
[19:00] Uh, so the thing that that I came up in the beginning was just uh add signature on all the shipping. So it was around two bucks um or 225 to add the the signature for all the shipping which it was costing me per month at the rate that we're growing and the and the orders that we did last month $10,600 you know.
[19:25] Uh just cost extra cost for shipping just to do the signature which is like crazy. Um, so that's why I decided to to see how many orders or how many people were actually asking for another replacement or people that were asking or saying that they didn't receive their package and it was only like maybe probably 30 people, you know, or 40 people, you know.
[19:48] So that's why I decided to like remove the shipping cost, you know, that signature at the signature thing and just every time that somebody will say that they received the package, I will just do like one line saying like, hey, try to reach out USPS to contact them.
[20:08] But if they say again, no, I contact them but they say it's delivered but I don't have it. I'll just send them a replacement. You know, it look it looks good as a business because it says that you stand by your customers, but actually in the back end it's cheaper.
[20:25] Because if you divide $10,000 600 by $50, which is the cost for the faja, I should be doing 212 orders to to match the rate I was paying before. I only do 30 30 orders per month, you know?
[20:42] So, it's actually costing me like what like $1,500 per month just to do the extra replacements, you know. So I'm saving like probably eight grand just doing that, you know, and it looks good as a business, you know, because you're standing by customers.
[21:00] Um, so yeah, so always do the math on that. Uh, so it can be good for you.
[21:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lead Squeeze Strategy - Multi-Brand Funnel"

[21:32] As you can tell, uh those leads that comes in, we first offer them the faja shapewear. And then they we offer them a surgery um surgery vitamins that they need, you know, uh that's another company that that we launched right now.

[21:55] Uh which is like um they sell hemoglobin boosters and then arnica, which are two things that that are very needed for plastic surgery. One for the swelling and one for the recovery.
[22:10] Uh because if you have your hemoglobin very low, uh you won't get the surgery, right? So that's a very good pain point that we tell our our customers saying, "Hey, um are you willing to invest just 50 $50 to get this these pills that will increase your hemoglobin, you know.
[22:32] And then we're ensure that you will have surgery because the worst thing that can happen to you is that you get the call from your coordinator and say, "Hey, your hemoglobin levels are super low. You can't have surgery and everything that you did is needs to be rebooked, you know."
[22:50] Uh so it's a very super strong pain point. And then they appreciate it because it's the same niche, right? So first we offer them the faja the shapewear. Then they tell them hey we have a partnership with another company that sells vitamins that can help you um you know assure your surgery and also your recovery.
[23:12] You want me to refer them to you? It's actually the same salesperson right but we say that right and they say yes. Oh okay perfect. And then we call them again with the the different name but it's the same person. We offer them the we we offer we offer them the the surgery and vitamins.

[23:55] So, each company is actually paying like, I don't know, 80 cents, you know. Uh so, it makes total sense, right? [25:38] Um, as benefits goes, uh, I think that the main benefit that they have is actually only that that we can have access to the liquid checkout page that we can modify our checkout page however we want, you know.
[25:55] Um, besides that, everything else is is is actually honestly not that great. you know, I haven't had any support or like um actually like a rep like asking me how it's going or teaching me what's the features are.
[26:12] Uh but I honestly wanted to do it for a checkout page because I like how I know like big companies like uh Gym Shark or Purple has it which it looks like there are no Shopify but there are because they have Shopify Plus and they can like edit a liquid checkout page, you know.
[26:32] Uh so that's why I did it, you know. But just so you know guys know it's it's just $2,000 a month till you hit $800,000 uh $800,000 of revenue. Above that I think it's uh 20% or something like that, you know. But the main one is $2,000 per month.
[26:52] [Interação com audiência sobre percentual]

[27:25] And then automatically all your products will get down 20% like in the moment right instead of you going by one by one right uh you have unlimited stuff uh and you get like 2.1 u credit card processor fees instead of 2.4 before.
[27:42] So if you actually making like at least like $800, $900,000 per month, it doesn't actually cost $2,000 because you're saving like.3%. So it's actually more like $1,000, you know.
[27:58] Uh so by $1,000 probably you can justify it with a with a checkout page. Uh because you can actually get a lot of conversion rates optimization with that, you know, because you can modify it as you want and you can add like upsells inside the checkout part, you know, um which is good.
[28:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Shopify Capital Lending Offer"
NÚMEROS: Oferecido: $910,000, payback: $1,280,000, taxa: 17% flat, pagamento diário: 17% das vendas
VISUAL: Screenshot da oferta de capital do Shopify, breakdown de valores, estrutura de pagamento automático, comparação com lending tradicional
[28:22] Um Shopify Capital, I don't know if you know about that. Shopify Capital is a way to get funding like in the moment. Uh so they're offering me like over a million dollars uh $910,000 uh on my store.
[28:40] Uh of course the rate is super high. They're asking 17% payback as a flat rate but sometimes like business businesses like like mine which is only like 15 months in. Uh it's very hard to get lending you know.
[28:58] Um sometimes you have to do have like two to three years of records and taxes in order to get lending at this kind of volume you know. Um, and with these guys, it's just easy, you know. You just click there and then next day you have it in your bank account, you know.
[29:18] Um, they take out 17% of your sales every single day until you pick the loan back. You know, in that case, can you see uh $910,000, you need to pay back $1,280,000 uh with a with a fee, right? And they take it every single day.
[29:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Shopify Capital Experience"
NÚMEROS: Empréstimo anterior: $500,000, aprovação: next day, dinheiro: 2 dias
VISUAL: Timeline de aprovação, processo simplificado vs banco tradicional, destaque para ausência de garantia pessoal, uso para inventory
[29:42] Um, it can make sense if if you guys uh are looking for lending. Like I said, um it's not it's not very easy to get traditional lending if you're super new. Um, and like banks, I realize that they don't trust a lot in e-commerce brands because there's a lot of frauds and stuff.
[30:02] Uh, but Shopify is is just easier, right? Um, so if you need the money, uh, at least you have the option, right? Um, we actually took a loan before of of half a million dollars and it was, like I said, easy.
[30:20] Uh, no personal guarantee. They didn't ask about anything. You just click on it, the next day you get approved and then in two days you get in your bank like that, you know. Uh, so it's good uh to buy inventory.
[30:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Avalara Sales Tax Automation"
NÚMEROS: $56 per filing, setup fee: $3,000, 15 estados com nexus
VISUAL: Logo Avalara, mapa dos EUA destacando estados com obrigação fiscal, integração com Shopify Plus, automação de filing, comparação de custos vs CPA
[30:40] Uh Avalara is another thing that is like a maybe like a pro tip that you guys can have. Uh when you're uh at that kind of volume of sales, I didn't realize it. You need to start reporting sales taxes in every single state in the US, you know, which is a pain in the ass actually.
[31:00] Uh because you need to file taxes in every single file sales taxes in every single state that you have like a nexus what they call it, right? Uh so we are liable for right now for 15 states.
[31:15] Um, so I was just going crazy like how are they going to do this, you know, because so much paperwork. My CPA was telling me like we need like two full-time people doing that, you know. I was like that's crazy, you know.
[31:30] And then I just found this app which is like integrated integrated with Shopify Plus to get exactly how much money you need to pay every single month and they do the filing for you, you know.
[31:45] It's actually super cheap. They charge $56 per filing. Um, which is super cheap comparing with my CPA wanted to charge me. And they they have like a initiation fee with three grand. So they can apply for the sales taxes in every single state.
[32:05] Um, but they will take everything from you, you know. So it's a super good um it's it's worth you know because a fine uh for sales taxes uh for not paying that is it can be like very big, you know. So we need to pay uh the sales taxes, you know.
[32:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sales Tax Example"
NÚMEROS: Venda: $747,000, Sales tax: ~$10,000
VISUAL: Exemplo de cálculo de impostos, breakdown de valor de venda vs tax coletado, processo de pagamento mensal/trimestral por estado
[32:30] You can pay every month or every quarterly. That depends on the state, you know. So that's why also you need to integrate with the Shopify Plus. Uh but they will do everything for you. You just put the the bank on file and then it will just take your money every time and they would make you a copy of of the receipt, you know.
[32:50] So sometimes when you see like you sell $747,000 actually 747, you know, this is like around like 10 10k on sales taxes over there, you know. Um that we need we need to send them to them.
[33:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Worldwide Express Shipping Deal"
NÚMEROS: USPS current: $8-$12, Worldwide Express offer: $5-$6, economia: ~50%
VISUAL: Comparação de custos de shipping, logo UPS/Worldwide Express, contacto da representante, destaque para economia significativa e suporte dedicado

[33:32] Pretty much he's offering me he just put in proposal to me and then I'm pretty much accepting her uh new rates for to switch my whole shipping uh process to UPS uh at a lower cost like super lower cost like almost like 50% less than Shopify can offer me you know.
[33:55] Uh so just to so you guys know for example like a regular shipping with USPS through Shopify or to ship station which is the other one big that that somebody like e-commerce use I can be like around for our product around like eight to 12 bucks, you know, without signature.
[34:15] Uh she's offering me to me like for around six to or five to six bucks, you know, guarantee for every shipment. So, I will save a lot of money if that deal goes through. I just wanted to give the information if you want to use her too.

[34:55] So that's why Worldwide Express is taking care of that department like e-commerce or small business uh uh to actually compete with USPS right uh so that's why they have so so um that good of rates right.
[35:12] And then you have a person that you can contact directly if you have any issues usually I mean pretty much everyone that has like a a e-commerce store knows that USPS can be like super painful uh with the feedback you know.
[35:28] They never reply they they you go to the USPS center and they they don't want to talk to you you know even though you you send a lot of shipments with them, you know. Um sometimes that the car didn't comes to pick up your packages. So, it's just a hustle, you know.
[35:45] Uh at least we heard we have like a direct person or rep at UPS that can might make uh anything that we need um from us, you know. So, yeah. So, I just want to give you that guys.

[37:32] Uh because I I get questions all the time like why you don't own a house like my family and parents, right? I I want that, right? But they all know this, right? So, I rather spend my money just buying uh building u brands or like buying more businesses like that.
[37:52] Um either flip it or just keep them, you know? Uh but I know that at least I have like a cushioning, right? Uh there. So, definitely uh you guys can see uh the Shopify stores or like brands in general can be definitely like a another form of investments, you know.
[38:10] Um I think that's pretty much it, you know. I know. Yeah.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Receita Total: $6+ milhões em 15 meses
Receita Mensal (último mês): $800,000 Shopify + $40,000 Amazon = $840,000
Valuation: $10 milhões (E-commerce Flipper)
Produto: Fajas pós-cirúrgicas ($129.99-$160 vs $40-60 mercado)
Campanhas Facebook: 3 principais rodando há 1 ano sem alterações
CAC Facebook: $37-$50
Taxa de Retorno: Significativamente abaixo dos 10% da indústria
CANAIS DE RECEITA ADICIONAIS:
Klaviyo (Email Marketing): $187,000/mês
PayPal: $150,000/mês
Afiliados (10% comissão): $44,000/mês
Sales Team (2 pessoas): $35,000/mês | CAC: $2/lead → ~$20/aquisição
Express Shipping: $15,000/mês (lucro: ~$12,000)
One Click Upsell (pós-compra): $13,000/mês
In-Cart Upsell: $68,000 add-to-cart
Sales Pop: ~$30,000-40,000/mês
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
1. Produto Premium:
6 meses de desenvolvimento focado em qualidade
Research baseado em reviews da Amazon
Preço 2-3x acima do mercado justificado por qualidade superior
2. Multi-Offer Funnel (Squeeze the Lead):
Lead cost: $2 ÷ 3 ofertas = $0.67 por marca
Sequência: Fajas → Surgery Vitamins ($50) → Post-op Massages
Plano de verticalizar com centro próprio de massagens
3. Matemática de Ofertas:
Acessório grátis ($39.99 retail, $3 custo) > 20% desconto ($26 perda)
Economia de $23 por transação + melhor percepção de valor
4. Otimização de Shipping:
Removeu assinatura obrigatória ($2.25/pedido = $10,600/mês)
Substituiu por replacement policy (~30-40 casos = $1,500/mês)
Economia: $8,000/mês + melhora reputação
5. Payment Diversification:
PayPal essencial para demografia (apesar de resistência)
Shop Payments (Afterpay/Klarna) como diferencial
Múltiplos métodos aumentam conversão
6. Sales Team Consultivo:
Script: resolver objeções sobre compra online + orientação de tamanho
Reduz returns através de consultoria pré-venda
Plano: escalar de 2 para 30-40 vendedores
ESTRUTURA OPERACIONAL:
Shopify Plus ($2,000/mês):
Benefício principal: Liquid Checkout customization
Fee reduction: 2.4% → 2.1% (economia parcial do custo)
Features: Flow, Launchpad, unlimited staff
Threshold: $800,000 revenue → depois percentual
Avalara (Sales Tax):
$56/filing + $3,000 setup
Automação de 15 estados
Economia vs 2 funcionários full-time
Shopify Capital:
Oferecido: $910,000 (payback $1,280,000 = 17% flat)
Pagamento: 17% das vendas diárias até quitar
Aprovação instantânea, dinheiro em 2 dias
Usado para inventory
Worldwide Express:
Negociando: $5-6/shipment vs $8-12 atual
Economia de ~50% em shipping
Rep dedicado vs zero suporte USPS
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Investimento Inicial: Não especificado
Tempo até $1M: ~4-5 meses (baseado em gráfico)
Margem Bruta: Alta (produto $129-160, custo não revelado mas permite 50%+ markup em acessórios)
LTV: Expandido através de multi-offer funnel
Exit Potential: $6-10 milhões (15 meses de operação)
FILOSOFIA DE NEGÓCIO:
E-commerce como veículo de investimento vs ativos tradicionais
Foco em construir/comprar múltiplas marcas
Estratégia de flip ou hold baseado em performance
Priorizar crescimento do negócio sobre consumo pessoal
DURAÇÃO: ~38 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação ao vivo para Inner Circle (primeira apresentação do palestrante) NICHO: Post-surgical shapewear (fajas) com expansão planejada para everyday use
